canonical_interface.oracle_model: Coherent indexed Bernoulli-rotation oracle B_theta and its inverse/controlled forms, with B_theta|i>|0> = |i>(cos(theta_i)|0> + sin(theta_i)|1>), theta_i in [0, pi/2], and mean mu_i = sin^2(theta_i); mean order is monotone in theta_i and neither angles nor means are directly readable.
canonical_interface.output_relation: Within atomic_query_cap, return either the complete exact Top-k index set or INCONCLUSIVE. The probability of returning an incorrect index set is at most delta. No completeness guarantee is made at a fixed cap without a public gap lower bound; ties and non-promise instances must fail closed as INCONCLUSIVE.
canonical_interface.query_unit: One call to B_theta, B_theta_dagger, controlled-B_theta, or controlled-B_theta_dagger; powers are charged by multiplicity.

primary_source.title: Modified Iterative Quantum Amplitude Estimation is Asymptotically Optimal
primary_source.theorem_locator: Main MIQAE correctness and query-complexity theorem in the cited version

baselines.oracle_model: Single-amplitude state-preparation oracle A, Grover iterate powers, and measurements; one independent instance is compiled for each indexed arm.
baselines.output_relation: Simultaneous confidence intervals for every arm, followed by exact Top-k sorting only when the kth and (k+1)th intervals are disjoint.
interface_assumptions: B_theta can be index-fixed to a charged per-arm state-preparation oracle
failure probability is allocated across all arms and adaptive rounds
the strict boundary is discovered rather than supplied
baselines.query_bound_template: sum_i Q_MIQAE(epsilon_i, delta_i), with every Grover power charged and sum_i delta_i <= delta; instantiate epsilon_i until the Top-k boundary certifies
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: It can return the complete exact Top-k set after a blind, charged per-arm compilation.

baselines.display_name: Rall coherent amplitude estimation with rounding promise

primary_source.title: Faster Coherent Quantum Algorithms for Phase, Energy, and Amplitude Estimation
primary_source.theorem_locator: Definition 1 rounding promise and coherent estimator results

baselines.oracle_model: Controlled eigenphase/amplitude unitary and inverse under an explicit finite-bit rounding promise, with diamond-norm error control.
baselines.output_relation: Coherently write rounded amplitude or phase bits; a separate legal selection composition must convert them to complete exact Top-k output.
interface_assumptions: all supported phases satisfy the declared rounding promise
controlled oracle powers and inverse calls are available
garbage and phase errors are included in the composition analysis
baselines.query_bound_template: O(2^b * alpha^(-1) * log(1/eta)) controlled-unitary calls for b output bits, rounding margin alpha, and channel error eta, before Top-k composition
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: It is a mandatory coherent-estimation threat but does not by itself return a Top-k set.

baselines.display_name: Wang-You-Li-Childs QBAI with repeated winner removal

primary_source.title: Quantum Exploration Algorithms for Multi-Armed Bandits
primary_source.theorem_locator: Best-arm identification main theorem and matching lower bound

baselines.oracle_model: Coherent indexed reward-state oracle encoding Bernoulli means as amplitudes.
baselines.output_relation: One best arm with fixed confidence per invocation; complete Top-k requires k legal invocations with coherent exclusion or deflation and global certification.
interface_assumptions: reward oracle matches the coherent bandit access model
already returned arms can be excluded without free hidden memory
round failure probabilities compose to at most delta
baselines.query_bound_template: sum_{r=1}^k tilde-O(sqrt(sum_{i in remaining_r excluding winner_r} Delta_{r,i}^(-2))), including exclusion and verification costs
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: Repeated legal QBAI is a direct same-output-relation competitor once all k-round costs are charged.

primary_source.title: Quantum Approximate k-Minimum Finding
primary_source.theorem_locator: Theorems 5.2 and 6.1 for weak and strong approximate k-minimum index sets

baselines.oracle_model: Coherent approximate-value oracle returning superpositions of epsilon-close values with bounded error, plus a dynamic set data structure.
baselines.output_relation: Weak or strong approximate k-minimum index set; exact Top-k follows only after precision is related to the unknown strict boundary gap.
interface_assumptions: a charged approximate-value oracle is compiled from B_theta
the dynamic set implementation does not receive free QRAM
the approximation guarantee is strong enough to resolve exact Top-k
baselines.query_bound_template: Q_GJW(n, k, epsilon, delta; O_approx) from Theorems 5.2/6.1, multiplied by the charged B_theta cost of O_approx and instantiated at boundary-resolving epsilon
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: The strong approximate k-minimum relation can become exact Top-k when a legally discovered precision resolves the boundary.

baselines.oracle_model: Step-query access to variable-time procedures with unknown stopping times and constant-factor upper estimates for aggregate and maximum time parameters.
baselines.output_relation: Detect or find a marked branch; exact Top-k needs repeated or all-output composition and a generated marking predicate.
interface_assumptions: each arm computation is exposed step by step as a reversible procedure
stopping is recognizable without revealing the hidden answer
required aggregate-time estimates are obtained without free instance knowledge
baselines.query_bound_template: Theta(sqrt(T * log(min(n, t_max)))) step queries for unknown-time VTS, T = sum_i t_i^2, before complete-output composition
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: It is the mandatory optimal unknown-time search threat; output conversion remains necessary.

baselines.oracle_model: Outer quantum algorithm invoking variable-cost subroutines on superpositions, with query weights and expected subroutine times.
baselines.output_relation: Inherited from a fully specified outer algorithm; no Top-k relation is supplied by the framework alone.
interface_assumptions: outer query weights are defined for the actual Top-k algorithm
subroutine expected costs include all oracle and inverse calls
composition errors and cleanup are controlled
baselines.query_bound_template: Q_outer * sum_i q_i E[T_i] for straight-line composition, instantiated with actual query weights, subroutine costs, and errors
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: Any claimed new nested quantum primitive must first exclude this published composition framework on the same outer relation.

primary_source.title: Loop Composition in Quantum Algorithms
primary_source.theorem_locator: Loop-composition theorem and variable-time-search recovery construction

baselines.oracle_model: Quantum outer loop with branches and composed variable-cost subroutines represented through program composition.
baselines.output_relation: Inherited from a concrete loop program; a complete Top-k loop and its durable-output semantics must be instantiated.
interface_assumptions: the frontier update is expressible as a legal quantum loop
every branch predicate and loop invocation has a charged implementation
durable outputs survive inverse cleanup without free history
baselines.query_bound_template: Q_loop-composition(outer loop, subroutine profiles, errors) from the cited loop theorem after an explicit Top-k program instantiation
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: It directly threatens novelty claims based only on nested or repeated frontier loops.

primary_source.title: Quantum Linear System Algorithm with Optimal Queries to Initial State Preparation
primary_source.theorem_locator: Section 3 Tunable Variable Time Amplitude Amplification

baselines.oracle_model: Nested amplitude-amplification stages with tunable thresholds, potentially-good subspaces, known norm/success parameters or a charged relaxation.
baselines.output_relation: Amplify a designated success subspace; exact Top-k requires a complete frontier predicate and multi-output construction.
interface_assumptions: nested success projectors are coherently implementable
threshold and success-amplitude information is available or discovered at cost
state preparation, reflection, and inverse calls are all counted
baselines.query_bound_template: Q_TunableVTAA(stage costs, success amplitudes, threshold schedule) from Section 3, instantiated rather than replaced by an RMS proxy
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: It is the mandatory optimized nested-amplification comparator for the proposed frontier unitary.

primary_source.title: Basic Quantum Subroutines: Finding Multiple Marked Elements and Summing Numbers

baselines.oracle_model: Boolean marking oracle over N items and small quantum memory, with exactly k marked elements to enumerate.
baselines.output_relation: Return all k marked elements; exact Top-k follows only when a legal generated predicate marks exactly the hidden Top-k set.
interface_assumptions: a coherent exact Top-k membership predicate has already been generated
the marked count or its required handling is available legally
small quantum memory operations are explicitly compiled
baselines.query_bound_template: O(sqrt(N*k)) calls to the supplied marking predicate plus polylogarithmic gate overhead; multiply by predicate construction and uncomputation cost
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: It is the optimal complete-output threat once a legal membership predicate exists.

primary_source.title: Quantum Query Complexity of Some Graph Problems
primary_source.theorem_locator: Theorem 3.4, exact-value k-minimum subroutine used in the graph algorithms

baselines.oracle_model: Exact coherent value oracle supporting comparisons of unordered elements.
baselines.output_relation: Return the k smallest exact values with optimal-order quantum queries.
interface_assumptions: exact binary arm values are available coherently
comparisons do not pay amplitude-estimation precision cost
ties follow the exact-value source convention
baselines.query_bound_template: Theta(sqrt(N*k)) exact-value oracle queries in the standard exact-value k-minimum setting
baselines.primary_eligibility_reason: Exact values reveal strictly more information than the canonical amplitude oracle, so this is a lower-envelope control rather than a primary competitor.
